briefdescription.para: spike data of sender node, in some cases required to retrieve node ID

detaileddescription.para:
The sender port is used as a unique identifier for the connection. The receiver of an event can use the port number to obtain data from the sender. The sender uses this number to locate target-specific information.
A negative port number indicates an unknown port.

The receiver port (r-port) can be used by the receiving Node to distinguish incoming connections. E.g. the r-port number can be used by Events to access specific parts of a Node. In most cases, however, this port will no be used.
The use of this port number is optional.
An r-port number of 0 indicates that the port is not used.
Number of simulations steps that pass before the event is delivered at the receiver. The delay must be at least 1.
The time stamp specifies the absolute time when the event shall arrive at the target.
Caches the value of stamp in steps for efficiency. Needs to be declared mutable since it is modified by a const function (get_rel_delivery_steps).
offset_ specifies a correction to the creation time. If the resolution of stamp is not sufficiently precise, this attribute can be used to correct the creation time. offset_ has to be in [0, h).
This operator calls the handler for the specific event type at the receiver.

briefdescription.para: Return node ID of receiving Node.

simplesect.para: This will cause a segmentation fault if sender has not been set via set_sender().

detaileddescription.para: Return node ID of sending Node.

simplesect.para: This will trigger an assertion if sender node id has not been set.

detaileddescription.para: Retrieve node ID of sending Node from SourceTable and return it.

briefdescription.para: Change node ID of sending Node.

memberdef.argsstring: (const size_t tid, const synindex syn_id, const size_t lcid)
memberdef.name: set_sender_node_id_info

briefdescription.para: Set tid, syn_id, lcid of spike_data_.

detaileddescription.para: These are required to retrieve the Node ID of a non-local sender from the SourceTable.

briefdescription.para: Return time stamp of the event.

detaileddescription.para:
The stamp denotes the time when the event was created. The resolution of Stamp is limited by the time base of the simulation kernel (
class nest::Time). If this resolution is not fine enough, the creation time can be corrected by using the time attribute.

briefdescription.para: Set the transmission delay of the event.

detaileddescription.para:
The delay refers to the time until the event is expected to arrive at the receiver.
t
delay.

briefdescription.para: Return transmission delay of the event.

detaileddescription.para: The delay refers to the time until the event is expected to arrive at the receiver.

briefdescription.para: Relative spike delivery time in steps.

detaileddescription.para: Returns the delivery time of the spike relative to a given time in steps. Causality commands that the result should not be negative.

briefdescription.para: Return the sender port number of the event.

detaileddescription.para:
This function returns the number of the port over which the Event was sent.
A
negative return value indicates that no port number was available.

briefdescription.para: Return the receiver port number of the event.

detaileddescription.para:
This function returns the number of the r-port over which the Event was sent.
A return value of 0 indicates that the r-port is not used.

Each event carries the number of the port over which the event is sent. When a connection is established, it receives a unique ID from the sender. This number has to be stored in each Event object.
p
Port number of the connection, or -1 if unknown.

When a connection is established, the receiving Node may issue a port number (r-port) to distinguish the incomin connection. By the default, the r-port is not used and its port number defaults to zero.
p
Receiver port number of the connection, or 0 if unused.

briefdescription.para: Return the creation time offset of the Event.

detaileddescription.para: Each Event carries the exact time of creation. This time need not coincide with an integral multiple of the temporal resolution. Rather, Events may be created at any point in time.

briefdescription.para: Set the creation time of the Event.

detaileddescription.para:
Each Event carries the exact time of creation in realtime. This time need not coincide with an integral multiple of the temporal resolution. Rather, Events may be created at any point in time.
t
Creation time in realtime. t has to be in [0, h).

briefdescription.para: Set drift_factor of the event (see DiffusionConnectionEvent).
Set diffusion_factor of the event (see DiffusionConnectionEvent).
Returns true if the pointer to the sender node is valid.
Returns true if the pointer to the receiver node is valid.

detaileddescription.para: This function returns true, if all data, in particular sender and receiver pointers are correctly set.

briefdescription.para: Set the time stamp of the event.

detaileddescription.para: The time stamp refers to the time when the event was created.

Event is the base class for transmitting information between nodes in NEST, with different subclasses for transmitting different types of information. Event types come in three categories
SpikeEvent can be transmitted between MPI processes
SecondaryEvent subclasses can also be transmitted between MPI processes, but need to be transmitted via secondary connections. They can transport data.
All other Event subclasses can only be transmitted within an MPI process

Events are used for two tasks:
Creating connections
Sending signals between nodes during simulation

sect2.title: Events during connection
sect2.para: Node::send_test_event() creates an Event instance of the type of event emitted by that node type, and calls Node::handles_test_event() on the target node. During this call, the event will contain a pointer to a sender node, which is not necessarily the actual sender (which may reside on a different MPI rank), but usually a proxy node. The sender node id is not set. The essential task of this handshake is to ensure that the target can handle the connection and requested receptor type, and to return rport information.

sect2.title: Events during simulation
sect2.para: Events transmit information during simulation. SpikeEvent and SecondaryEvent types are first stored in buffers on the sending VP, then serialized for transmission to destination VPs and finally deserialized for delivery. In this process, for the sake of efficiency, NEST creates one Event object and updates its properties for each single event to be delivered. In this case, no pointer to the source node is stored in the Event (as it may be on a different MPI rank), but the correct sender node id is provided.
Other Event types are delivered directly on the VP on which they are generated and can, for example, be used for call backs or request-reply sequences.
